topic.name: Oil and Gas Regulation
topic.description: Legislation governing royalties and environmental protections related to oil and gas production on the Outer Continental Shelf.

Document 1: Amends the Outer Continental Shelf Lands Act to declare that any royalty under an oil and gas lease... shall apply to all oil that is removed under the lease and discharged into waters of the United States...
Increases the minimum royalty under such leases from 12.5% to 20%.

topic.name: Renewable Energy Tax Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on various amendments to the Internal Revenue Code related to tax incentives and credits for renewable energy and related activities.

Document 1: income tax credit for biodiesel and renewable diesel
used as fuel
Document 2: exclude from gross income
value of any medal awarded in Olympic Games
Document 3: Fair Allocation of Internal Revenue Credit for Renewable Electricity Distribution
allow an Indian tribe...to assign to any other person
Document 4: expand the purposes for which tax-exempt facility bonds may be issued
include renewable energy resource facilities
Document 5: terminate the excise tax on diesel fuel
income tax credit for alcohol used as fuel
Document 6: refund a portion of the excise tax paid by certain coal producers
on coal exported
Document 7: extends through 2013
the Economic Growth and Tax Relief Reconciliation Act of 2001

topic.name: Education Policy and Reform
topic.description: This cluster discusses various legislative acts aimed at improving education systems, including funding for dual enrollment, school accountability, and teacher effectiveness.

Document 1: authorizes grants to support dual enrollment programs
requires no tuition fees for postsecondary courses
Document 2: prohibits federal funds to institutions admitting illegal aliens
Document 3: awards competitive grants to support school reform efforts
addresses areas such as teacher effectiveness and improving data usage
Document 4: requires science assessments in national academic achievement tests
establishes standards for mathematics and science education
Document 5: replaces charter school grant program with a competitive grant program
requires states to ensure accountability in charter school operations
Document 6: requires criminal background checks for school employees
authorizes use of funds to improve background check policies

topic.name: Medicare and Medicaid Legislation
topic.description: Legislation related to the expansion and modification of Medicare and Medicaid services and coverage.

Document 1: Amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act to extend Medicare coverage to medical nutrition therapy services for people with pre-diabetes
risk factors for developing type-2 diabetes.
Document 2: Amends the U.S. Troop Readiness, Veterans' Care, Katrina Recovery, and Iraq Accountability Appropriations Act, 2007
to delay for six months the effective date of the requirement to use tamper-resistant prescription pads under title XIX (Medicaid) of the Social Security Act.
Document 3: Amends part D (Voluntary Prescription Drug Benefit Program) of title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act
to require application to specialty tier drugs of the process for exception from the tiered cost-sharing.
Document 4: Amends title XIX (Medicaid) of the Social Security Act to require Medicaid drug coverage of drugs prescribed for treatment of obesity.
to require Medicaid drug coverage of drugs prescribed for treatment of obesity.
Document 5: Amends part B (Supplementary Medical Insurance Benefits for Aged and Disabled) of title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act
to repeal the Medicare competitive acquisition program for durable medical equipment and prosthetics, orthotics, and supplies.
Document 6: Amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act to repeal the exclusion of residents of Puerto Rico from deemed enrollment under part B
to provide for a special seven-month enrollment period for such residents.
Document 7: Amends the Consolidated Omnibus Budget Reconciliation Act of 1985

to add health insuring organizations operated by public entities established by Ventura and Merced Counties, California, to the number of county-organized health insuring organizations in California authorized to enroll Medicaid beneficiaries.

topic.name: Tariff Suspension Amendments
topic.description: The documents discuss amendments to the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States regarding temporary suspensions of duties on various goods.

Document 1: suspend temporarily the duty on certain sleeping bags.
Document 2: suspend temporarily the duty on poly(1,6-diisocyanatohexane-block-polyethylene-block-poly-propylene glycol monobutyl ether).
Document 3: extend the temporary suspension of duty on certain cathode-ray tubes.
Document 4: suspend temporarily the duty on certain work footwear for men.
Document 5: extend the temporary suspension of duty on formulations containing Diuron and application adjuvants.
Document 6: suspend temporarily the duty on certain structures, parts, and components for use in an isotopic separation facility in southern Ohio.
Document 7: extend the temporary suspension of duty on certain acrylic staple fibers.

topic.name: Veterans Affairs Legislation
topic.description: This cluster encompasses various legislative measures aimed at improving access to healthcare and benefits for veterans.

Document 1: WINGMAN Act directs VA to provide veterans' records access to congressional employees.
Opportunity to permit designated congressional employee to have read-only access to veteran's records.
Document 2: Amends the Veterans Access, Choice, and Accountability Act of 2014.
Makes the VA Choice Program permanent.
Document 3: Permits use of telecommunications for military and veterans' healthcare under TRICARE.
Applies to veterans receiving health care benefits under VA.
Document 4: Improvement of Access to Care from Non-Department of Veterans Affairs Providers.
Requires care for veterans unable to schedule appointments at VA facilities.
Document 5: Increases grant amounts for home improvements for disabled veterans.
Amount authorized for veterans with service-connected disabilities increased.
Document 6: Adds Parkinson's disease to list of service-connected diseases for Vietnam veterans.
Veterans exposed to herbicides can qualify for benefits.
Document 7: Allows receipt of both military retired pay and veterans' disability compensation.
Makes full concurrent receipt available for certain service-connected disabilities.

topic.name: Victim Support and Rights
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation and policies aimed at protecting and supporting victims of domestic violence, sexual assault, and related crimes.

Document 1: includes as a homeless veteran, for purposes of eligibility for benefits through the Department of Veterans Affairs, a veteran or veteran's family who: (1) is fleeing domestic or dating violence, sexual assault, stalking, or other dangerous or life-threatening conditions
has no other residence and lacks the resources or support networks to obtain other permanent housing.
Document 2: requires institutions of higher education to include in their annual security report policies encouraging the accurate and prompt reporting of all crimes
requires schools to protect victim confidentiality when reporting criminal threats to the campus community.
Document 3: amends the Violence Against Women Act of 1994 to add or expand definitions of several terms used in such Act
modifies or expands grant conditions under such Act, including requirements relating to nondisclosure of personally identifying information or other client information.
Document 4: this bill amends the Indian Tribal Justice Act to rename the office supporting tribal justice systems and courts to the Office of Tribal Justice System Support and Victim Services
the office must make grants to Indian tribes for crime victim compensation programs and services for crime victims.
Document 5: prohibits denial or termination of housing assistance on the basis of being such a victim under specified federal housing programs
requires each owner or manager of housing assisted under a covered program to adopt an emergency transfer policy for tenants who are victims of domestic violence.
Document 7: authorizes the Attorney General to make grants to state and local governments for supervised visitation and safe visitation exchange of children and youth by and between parents in situations involving domestic violence
ensures that by January 1, 2016, a court-appointed special advocate shall be available to every victim of child abuse or neglect in the United States that needs one.

topic.name: Banking Regulation and Consumer Protection
topic.description: This topic encompasses various legislative measures aimed at regulating banking practices and protecting consumers in financial transactions.

Document 1: Directs the Comptroller General to study any bank holding company having more than $500 billion in consolidated assets
Defines 'economic benefit' as the difference between actual loan terms offered, debt or equity prices, or asset values
Document 2: Prohibits financial holding companies and national banks from engaging, directly or indirectly, in real estate brokerage or real estate management activities
Exempts from such prohibition activities of a bank holding company that directly relate to managing any real property owned
Document 3: Amends the Gramm-Leach-Bliley Act to exempt from its annual privacy policy notice requirement any financial institution
The federal banking agencies are directed to study jointly the appropriate capital requirements for mortgage servicing assets
Document 4: Amends the Federal Deposit Insurance Act to authorize any depository institution to lease to any individual for up to five years an interest in residential property
Directs the federal banking agencies to jointly prescribe specified safety and soundness regulations
Document 5: Prohibits prepayment fees or penalties under any consumer credit transaction secured by the consumer's owner-occupied principal dwelling
Document 6: Amends the Electronic Fund Transfer Act to extend its coverage to spending accounts established by a consumer at an insured depository institution
Treats as a spending account any similar deposit account operated or managed by a financial institution
Document 7: Amends the Truth in Lending Act to prohibit any creditor or mortgage broker from making a consumer credit transaction without verifying the consumer's reasonable ability to make the scheduled payments
Prescribes criteria for determining the reasonable ability to make such payments

topic.name: Tribal Land and Resource Management
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to the management, transfer, and development of land and resources by Native American tribes and federal authorities.

Document 1: reestablish the Royalty Policy Committee
advise Interior as it formulates policies and regulations regarding mineral production
Document 2: allows the Coquille Indian Tribe to transfer, lease, encumber, or convey land
Document 3: provides for the exchange of land for equal value
requires the Bureau of Land Management to administer the Sky Ranch parcel as public land
Document 4: directs the Secretary of the Interior to transfer land to the Hoh Indian Tribe
prohibits commercial, residential, or industrial buildings on such land
Document 5: authorizes the Secretary of the Interior to exchange land within the Blue Ridge Parkway
Document 6: directs the Secretary of the Interior to take specified lands into trust for the Santa Ynez Band of Chumash Mission Indians
prohibits gambling on the land
Document 7: provides Indian tribes with technical assistance in planning their energy resource development programs
expands eligibility for DOE energy development loan guarantees to tribal organizations

topic.name: Presidential Election Transparency
topic.description: Legislation aimed at increasing transparency in presidential elections by requiring candidates to disclose their tax returns and enhancing campaign finance regulations.

Document 1: require any candidate of a major party for the office of President to file with the Federal Election Commission (FEC) a copy of the candidate's income tax returns
the FEC shall request the Department of the Treasury to furnish the returns.
Document 2: require any candidate of a major party for the office of President to file with the Federal Election Commission (FEC) a copy of the candidate's income tax returns
the FEC shall request the Department of the Treasury to furnish the returns.
Document 3: require each candidate of a major party for the office of President to file with the Federal Election Commission (FEC) a copy of the candidate's income tax returns
the FEC shall request the Department of the Treasury to furnish the returns.
Document 4: Amends the Internal Revenue Code (IRC) to allow a refundable credit of 50% of qualified congressional House campaign contributions
Directs the Government by the People Oversight Commission, established by this Act, to launch a pilot program.
Document 5: Directs the Federal Trade Commission (FTC) to revise the do-not-call registry provisions of the Telemarketing Sales Rule
Defines a 'politically-oriented recorded message telephone call' as an outbound telephone call that plays a recorded message.
Document 6: Amends the Federal Election Campaign Act of 1971 to revise the requirement of a 48-hour notification of a campaign contribution
Requires Senate candidates to file designations, statements, and reports directly with the FEC.
Document 7: Amends the Federal Election Campaign Act of 1971 to revise the requirement of a 48-hour notification of a campaign contribution
Requires Senate candidates to file designations, statements, and reports directly with the FEC.

topic.name: Immigration Policy and Regulation
topic.description: The documents discuss various aspects of U.S. immigration policy, including visa waivers, employment eligibility, and benefits for specific groups of aliens.

Document 1: establish a visa waiver program for the United States Virgin Islands
Secretary to suspend the admission of nationals of a country
Document 2: deny a tax deduction for wages and benefits paid to or on behalf of an unauthorized alien
establish a program to share information that may lead to the identification of unauthorized aliens
Document 3: extend the eligibility period for benefits under title XVI for certain aliens
diversity immigrant VISA petition fee of $30
Document 4: require the Department of Health and Human Services or the Department of Homeland Security to provide information to the governor
custodial placement in such state of an unaccompanied alien child
Document 5: prohibits an office of the legislative branch from entering into a contract for goods or services
contractor employing individuals unless the contractor elects to participate in the basic pilot program
Document 6: establish an employment-based, conditional immigrant visa
terminate the status of a sponsored entrepreneur if not meeting requirements
Document 7: give priority to compensating states and state political subdivisions
located on the U.S. border with Canada or Mexico

topic.name: Intelligence Funding and Oversight
topic.description: This topic encompasses the authorization, funding, and oversight mechanisms related to U.S. intelligence activities and agencies.

Document 1: Authorizes appropriations for FY2009 for the conduct of intelligence and intelligence-related activities
Specifies that the amounts authorized and the authorized personnel ceilings as of September 30, 2009, for such activities are those specified in the classified Schedule of Authorizations.
Document 2: Directs the President to develop and transmit to the appropriate congressional committees, with intelligence support from the Director of National Intelligence, a comprehensive interagency strategy and implementation plan for long-term security and stability in Afghanistan.
Document 3: Authorizes appropriations for FY2007 for the conduct of intelligence and intelligence-related activities
Requires notification of the congressional intelligence committees on the use of such authority.
Document 4: Amends the Intelligence Reform and Terrorism Prevention Act of 2004 to expand the functions of the Privacy and Civil Liberties Oversight Board
Permits the Board to investigate such complaints, or transmit such complaints to any other executive agency or the congressional intelligence committees.
Document 6: Requires the Director of National Intelligence to report to the congressional intelligence and defense committees on the research, development, testing, and deployment programs of foreign countries relating to electromagnetic pulse (EMP) weapons
Directs that such report identify each country pursuing an EMP weapons program and describe the scope of each such program.
Document 7: Amends the National Security Act of 1947 to direct the head of an element of the intelligence community (IC) to provide to the Secretary of Defense any intelligence information obtained by such IC element
Requires the Secretary to report annually to Congress concerning the instances in which such information was provided and the response to the information.

topic.name: Regulation of Drugs and Food Safety
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures related to the regulation, safety, and labeling of drugs and food products under the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act.

Document 1: Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act (FFDCA) to revise requirements for the regulation of compounding drugs.
Prohibits the sale of a drug product by an entity other than the pharmacy or physician that compounded it.
Document 2: This bill amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to require the label of a drug containing an opioid to prominently state that the drug contains an opioid and addiction to the drug is possible.
Document 3: Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to deem any drug or device to be adulterated if it contains mercury intended for use in a dental filling.
Deems a device that contains mercury intended for use in a dental filling to be misbranded.
Document 4: Requires the Secretary of Health and Human Services to issue an order refusing admission into the United States of all imports of seafood or seafood products originating from a country or exporter which the Secretary determines do not meet food safety requirements under the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act.
Requires shipments denied admission to be clearly marked 'Refused Entry by the United States Government.'
Document 5: This bill amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to prohibit the sale of food that has been genetically engineered or contains genetically engineered ingredients, unless that information is clearly disclosed.
Labeling or advertising foods containing genetically engineered material as 'natural,' or using similar words, is prohibited.
Document 6: Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to require the Secretary of Health and Human Services to assess and collect fees for an abbreviated application for a generic new animal drug.
Sets forth the process for developing recommendations to present to Congress for the review of abbreviated applications for generic new animal drugs.
Document 7: Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to require the Department of Health and Human Services (HHS) to establish a program to approve an antibacterial drug intended to treat a serious medical condition.
Prohibits a lack of evidence of a favorable benefit-risk profile in a broader population from resulting in a denial of approval.

topic.name: Mental Health Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at improving mental health services, support for veterans, and suicide prevention strategies.

Document 1: Garrett Lee Smith Memorial Act Reauthorization Act of 2007
require such information, training, and assistance on suicide among all ages, particularly for youth.
Document 2: Mental Health Care Provider Retention Act of 2016
allows an individual who has been diagnosed with a mental health condition to continue receiving treatment.
Document 3: Projects for Assistance in Transition from Homelessness
provide specified services to individuals who are suffering from serious mental illness.
Document 4: Services, Education, and Rehabilitation for Veterans Act
to develop, implement, or enhance veteran's treatment courts.
Document 5: Services, Education, and Rehabilitation for Veterans Act
for programs that involve continuing judicial supervision over nonviolent offenders with substance abuse or mental health problems.
Document 7: Mental Health Reform Act of 2016
improve mental and substance use disorder services provided by the Department of Defense and the Department of Veterans Affairs.

topic.name: Biobased Energy and Agriculture Policy
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative efforts to promote biobased products, renewable energy, and support for farmers through various agricultural programs.

Document 1: federal procurement of biobased products
bioenergy program, including establishment of feedstock residue management
Document 2: increase, and extend through FY2012, funding for the biodiesel fuel education program
Document 3: revise labeling requirements for federal procurement of biobased products
separate authorization of appropriations for federal procurement
Document 4: extend assistance to disadvantaged or beginning farmers or ranchers
provide priority assistance for new farming opportunities
Document 5: revise exemptions from federal maximum driving and on-duty time motor carrier regulations for drivers transporting agricultural commodities
Document 6: extend national dairy market loss payments
increases the maximum payment quantity for all producers on a single dairy operation
Document 7: establish an Office of Urban Agriculture within the Department of Agriculture
authorize and provide funding for several of the programs

topic.name: Iran Sanctions Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents focuses on various U.S. legislative measures aimed at imposing sanctions on Iran for its nuclear, ballistic missile, and terrorism-related activities.

Document 1: Amends the Iran Sanctions Act of 1996

prohibit any correspondent account or a payable-through account opened and maintained in the United States by a foreign financial institution that has knowingly conducted or facilitated any significant financial transaction
Document 2: amends the Iran-Iraq Arms Non-Proliferation Act of 1992 to cover ballistic missile or related technology
require sanctions on Iran and related entities and persons for actions to acquire or develop ballistic missiles
Document 3: direct the President to impose three or more specified sanctions against a person that knowingly participates in a joint venture with respect to the development of petroleum resources outside of Iran
U.S. policy to prevent the Islamic Republic of Iran from acquiring a nuclear weapons capability
Document 4: expresses the sense of Congress that diplomatic efforts to address Iran's illicit nuclear efforts
additional measures should be adopted by the United States to prevent the diversion and transshipment of sensitive dual-use technologies to Iran
Document 6: directs the Departments of State, Defense, Treasury, and the Director of National Intelligence to submit a 10-year strategy to counter Iranian activities and threats
impose specified sanctions against a person identified by the Department of State in an annual report as having acted to undermine, or as posing a risk of undermining

topic.name: Retirement and Tax Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative amendments related to retirement savings, tax treatment, and social security benefits.

Document 1: waive minimum distribution requirements for tax-exempt retirement plans
calendar years 2013 and 2014
Document 2: amends the Internal Revenue Code to modify the tax treatment of qualified tuition programs
nonrefundable tax credit for contributions to a 529 plan
Document 3: amends title II of the Social Security Act
allocations to the Federal Disability Insurance Trust Fund
Document 4: directs the Secretary of the Treasury to make available a new federal income tax Form 1040SR
used even if income includes social security benefits
Document 5: require certain employers who do not maintain qualifying retirement plans
payroll deposit individual retirement account arrangement
Document 6: amends titles II and XVI of the Social Security Act
treat as a disability for OASDI and SSI disability payment purposes
Document 7: increases the sentences for health care fraud
expands the definition of "health care fraud"

topic.name: Transportation Security Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents discusses various legislative acts aimed at enhancing security and safety measures within the transportation sector, including rail, air, and highway systems.

Document 1: Directs the Under Secretary for Border and Transportation Security to complete a vulnerability assessment of freight and passenger rail transportation
Establishes the position of Federal Rail Security Manager
Document 2: Extends the effective period of final orders of the Secretary of Transportation regarding the eligibility of small communities for essential air service subsidies
Document 3: Directs the Secretary of Transportation to modify certain federal regulations to improve highway safety
Establishes a national database of information on the safety of highway-rail grade crossings
Document 4: Designates the airport traffic control tower located at Spokane International Airport
Document 5: Revises requirements with respect to the training of aliens and other individuals designated by the Secretary of Homeland Security
Document 6: Requires domestic or foreign air carriers and manufacturers of aircraft to disclose to the FAA any attempted or successful cyberattack
The FAA must report to Congress annually on attempted and successful cyberattacks
Document 7: Directs the Office of the Ombudsman of the Transportation Security Administration to record complaints from the public regarding TSA screening practices
Requires every Category X airport to have at least one TSA passenger advocate on duty during operating hours

topic.name: Fishery Management Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents discusses various legislative acts aimed at managing and conserving fishery resources in U.S. waters.

Document 1: Amends the Magnuson-Stevens Fishery Conservation and Management Act
adds the state of New York as a member of the New England Fishery Management Council.
Document 2: Directs the Mid-Atlantic Fishery Management Council to submit for approval by the Secretary of Commerce a modified fishery management plan
prohibits the allocation of catch quotas on a state-by-state basis using historical landings data.
Document 3: Directs the Secretary of Commerce to conduct a benchmark stock assessment of the Gulf of Mexico red snapper fishery
prohibits fishing in the Gulf of Mexico red snapper fishery before such report demonstrates the need for interim or final rules.
Document 4: Amends the Magnuson-Stevens Fishery Conservation and Management Act to require the Secretary of Commerce to annually make available to the New England Fishery Management Council
funding of Council research and monitoring priorities.
Document 5: Amends the Coral Reef Conservation Act of 2000 to direct the Secretary of Commerce to submit to specified congressional committees a national coral reef ecosystem action strategy.
authorizes the Secretary to make agreements with nonprofit organizations for public-private partnerships promoting coral reef ecosystem conservation.
Document 6: Directs the Secretary of Commerce to conduct a benchmark stock assessment of the Gulf of Mexico red snapper fishery
deems any interim rule promulgated prior to enactment of this Act that decreases the number of fishing days to have no force or effect.
Document 7: Requires the Department of Transportation to report to Congress on the program for disposal of government-owned merchant vessels
the bill repeals authority to scrap any vessel sold from the National Defense Reserve Fleet in an approved foreign market.

topic.name: Civil Rights and Anti-Discrimination Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on various legislative efforts aimed at preventing discrimination and promoting civil rights across multiple domains, including credit, employment, adoption, and wage equality.

Document 1: prohibit discrimination on account of sexual orientation or gender identity
amends the Equal Credit Opportunity Act
Document 2: no predispute arbitration agreement shall be valid if it requires arbitration of a dispute arising under any statute intended to protect civil rights
validity or enforceability of an agreement to arbitrate shall be determined by a court
Document 3: prohibits discrimination against prospective adoptive or foster parents based on sexual orientation or gender identity
requires technical assistance to ensure compliance with the Act
Document 4: prohibits a servicemember's deployment from being considered in the determination of a child's best interest
amends the Servicemembers Civil Relief Act
Document 5: denies the National Labor Relations Board any power to order an employer to restore or reinstate any work
applies the amendment to any complaint for which a final adjudication has not been made
Document 6: ensure that bid specifications do not discriminate against parties based on labor organization agreements
allows exemptions for public health or safety
Document 7: revises remedies for enforcement of prohibitions against sex discrimination in payment of wages
makes it unlawful to require an employee to sign a contract prohibiting wage disclosure

topic.name: Telecommunications Regulation
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative acts aimed at regulating various aspects of telecommunications services, including emergency services, number portability, broadcasting licenses, consumer rights, and internet access.

Document 1: Amends the Communications Act of 1934 to direct the Federal Communications Commission (FCC) to require that each commercial mobile service provider provide service for the universal emergency telephone number (911)
facilitate the sharing of such infrastructure, technology, and facilities.
Document 2: Amends the Communications Act of 1934 to declare that a provider of voice services has the duty to provide number portability
Requires designation of 9-1-1 as the universal emergency telephone number within the United States.
Document 3: Amends the Communications Act of 1934 to allow the renewal of a full-power analog television broadcasting license
Requires the FCC to award the authority to use the channel through competitive bidding.
Document 4: Renders invalid or unenforceable any pre-dispute arbitration agreement between an individual and a provider of commercial mobile service
Defines a 'pre-dispute arbitration agreement' as an agreement to arbitrate a dispute that has not yet arisen.
Document 5: Amends the Internet Tax Freedom Act to make permanent the ban on state and local taxation of Internet access
on multiple or discriminatory taxes on electronic commerce.
Document 6: Amends the Communications Act of 1934 to direct the FCC to implement a five-year pilot program which distributes funding to secondary schools
Requires the FCC to implement a pilot program that allows secondary schools to apply for meaningfully discounted services.
Document 7: Amends the Communications Act of 1934 to allow the FCC to authorize interim carriage of such station by the MVPD
Directs the FCC to complete a rulemaking proceeding to determine whether blocking of online content constitutes a failure to negotiate in good faith.

topic.name: Data Privacy and Security Legislation
topic.description: This topic encompasses various legislative measures aimed at protecting personal information and enhancing data security across different sectors.

Document 1: Makes it unlawful for any person who is not the owner or authorized user to engage in unfair or deceptive acts or practices in connection with specified conduct.
Requires reports.
Document 2: Amends the Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) to revise requirements for federal agency disclosures of information requested under that Act.
Directs the Attorney General to notify the Special Counsel of civil actions taken for arbitrary and capricious rejections of requests for agency records.
Document 3: Instructs the Director of Office of Management and Budget (OMB) to establish policies, procedures, and standards for agencies to follow in the event of a breach of data security.
Requires timely notification to individuals whose sensitive personal information could be compromised as a result of a breach.
Document 4: Prohibits a provider of remote computing service or electronic communication service to the public from knowingly divulging to a governmental entity the contents of any communication.
Requires the government to obtain a warrant from a court before requiring providers to disclose the content of such communications.
Document 5: Prohibits an American Health Benefit Exchange from requesting personal information of an individual through a website unless such information is necessary.
Requires any employee who has access to personal information to pass a criminal background check.
Document 6: Requires the Federal Trade Commission (FTC) to promulgate regulations requiring each person engaged in interstate commerce that owns or possesses electronic data containing personal information to establish security policies and procedures.
Prescribes procedures for notification to the FTC and affected individuals of information security breaches.
Document 7: Directs the Secretary of Health and Human Services to notify individuals within one hour after the discovery of a breach of personally identifiable information.
Requires the Secretary to report to Congress annually on all such breaches that occurred within the past year.

topic.name: Wildlife Conservation Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents focuses on legislative amendments and acts related to wildlife conservation, target practice, and the management of natural resources.

Document 1: amends the Pittman-Robertson Wildlife Restoration Act
facilitate the construction and expansion of public target ranges
Document 2: amends the Pittman-Robertson Wildlife Restoration Act
delay by 10 years until 2026 the date after which interest from the wildlife conservation and restoration fund is available for apportionment
Document 3: directs the Secretary of the Interior to issue a final decision on whether or not to issue a permit under the Endangered Species Act of 1973
deems the permit to have been issued if the Secretary does not make a final decision within 90 days
Document 4: amends the Endangered Species Act of 1973
prohibit any regulation that goes in effect after February 24, 2014, from prohibiting or restricting the possession, sale, delivery, receipt, shipping, or transportation of elephant ivory
Document 5: amends the Migratory Bird Treaty Act
permit the taking of any migratory game bird on or over land that is not a baited area
Document 6: amends the Pittman-Robertson Wildlife Restoration Act
delay by 10 years until 2026 the date after which interest from the wildlife conservation and restoration fund is available for apportionment
Document 7: grants the Secretary of the Interior permanent authority to authorize any state to issue electronic duck stamps
sets forth state electronic duck stamp application requirements

topic.name: Small Business Funding and Support
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation and programs aimed at enhancing financial access and support for small businesses, particularly focusing on loans, grants, and technical assistance.

Document 1: reauthorize certain small business assistance programs of the Small Business Administration (SBA)
increases the average microloan loan amount
Document 2: prohibit an insured credit union from making any member business loan that would result in exceeding specified limits
directs the Board to develop a tiered approval process for member business lending
Document 3: exempt from passive loss rules any activity of a taxpayer carried on by a high technology research small business pass-thru entity
Document 4: requires a report on the entrepreneurial impact of technology transfer at the National Laboratories
evaluation of metrics including the number of licenses granted to small businesses
Document 5: establish within the Small Business Administration (SBA) an Office of Minority Small Business Development
authorize the SBA Administrator to make grants to assist in establishing an entrepreneurship curriculum
Document 6: exempt from passive loss rules any activity of a taxpayer carried on by a high technology research small business pass-thru entity
Document 7: amends the Sarbanes-Oxley Act to revise the small issuer exemption
specifies that this requirement shall not apply to an issuer that has a total public float for the relevant reporting period of less than $1 billion

topic.name: Government Oversight and Regulation
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to government oversight, regulatory measures, and accountability in various sectors including housing, transportation, and public safety.

Document 1: Requires the Administrator of the Rural Utilities Service or the Assistant Secretary of Commerce for Communications and Information to terminate for cause any award
determine whether cause exists to terminate such award
Document 2: requires a public housing agency to contract to make section 8 rental assistance payments directly to a low-income family
if the owner of the real property refuses to enter into a contract
Document 3: directs the U.S. Customs and Border Protection (CBP) to report to Congress
an assessment of whether additional radiation detection equipment is necessary
Document 4: Requires the Secretaries of Commerce and Health and Human Services to execute a memorandum of understanding to improve interagency cooperation on seafood safety
Defines 'seafood fraud' as the mislabeling or misrepresentation of seafood
Document 5: authorizes the Department of the Army to produce, treat, manage, and use natural gas located under Fort Knox
may not be sold for use elsewhere
Document 6: Designates buildings occupied by the Department of Transportation as the James L. Oberstar United States Department of Transportation Building Complex
Document 7: Directs the Assistant Secretary of Homeland Security to establish in the TSA an aviation security advisory committee
Requires the Advisory Committee to develop, upon the Assistant Secretary's request, recommendations to improve aviation security

topic.name: Voting Rights and Election Integrity
topic.description: This cluster of documents addresses various legislative measures aimed at protecting voter rights, improving election processes, and ensuring transparency in campaign financing.

Document 1: establish notice and review requirements for removal of individuals from a list of eligible voters
prohibit a state from refusing to register any applicant who has completed the national voter registration application form
Document 2: quadruple the matching rate for contributions to primary election candidates
require presidential candidates to participate in the primary payment system to be eligible for general election payments
Document 3: reduce from $2,000 to $1,000 the maximum amount of contributions that any person may make to any candidate
requires adjustment to such amount for inflation
Document 4: provide mail voter registration application forms to each veteran
assisting in completing such applications and ballots
Document 6: prohibit an election official from requiring an individual to provide a photo identification
as a condition for receiving or casting a ballot in any election for federal office
Document 7: prohibit a corporation from engaging in any campaign-related activity if the corporation has been subject to any federal criminal penalty
a civil money penalty under this Act

topic.name: U.S. Trade Enforcement and Regulations
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at enforcing U.S. trade laws, particularly related to antidumping and countervailing duties, and regulating trade relationships, including with Cuba.

Document 1: Amends the Tariff Act of 1930 to eliminate the option of an importer to post a bond or security in lieu of a cash deposit for each entry of merchandise exported into the United States
Directs the Secretary of the Treasury to prescribe minimum standards to require customs brokers to implement reasonable procedures for collecting information
Document 2: Amends the Tariff Act of 1930 to apply the Act's antidumping and countervailing duty provisions to contracts and transactions of foreign countries
Document 3: Directs the U.S. Customs and Border Protection to provide for the reliquidation of, and the refund of collected excess antidumping duties
Document 4: Amends the Foreign Assistance Act of 1961 to repeal the embargo on trade with Cuba
Authorizes the President to impose export controls with respect to Cuba
Document 5: Amends the Tariff Act of 1930 to require the Commissioner responsible for U.S. Customs and Border Protection to initiate an investigation into claims of evasion of antidumping or countervailing duties
Document 6: Prohibits the President from restricting direct transfers from a Cuban depository institution to a U.S. depository institution in payment for a product authorized for sale
Directs the Secretary of Agriculture to provide information and technical assistance to U.S. agricultural producers
Document 7: Amends the Tariff Act of 1930 to authorize a petitioning party to bring a civil action in a U.S. district court for determination that a U.S. industry is materially injured by reason of imports

topic.name: Native Hawaiian Governance and Support
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to the governance, rights, and support for Native Hawaiians and other minority-serving institutions in the United States.

Document 1: Reaffirms that the United States has a special political and legal relationship with Native Hawaiians
Establishes the Native Hawaiian Interagency Coordinating Group
Document 2: Makes the following institutions eligible for assistance: a Native Hawaiian-serving institution
Strengthen the national digital and wireless infrastructure by increasing national investment in telecommunications
Document 3: Permits an eligible minority-serving institution to concurrently receive grant funds
Eligible minority-serving IHE is a Native Hawaiian-serving institution
Document 5: Establishes the U.S. Office for Native Hawaiian Relations
Reaffirms the special political and legal relationship between the United States and the Native Hawaiian people
Document 6: Establishes the Native American Economic Advisory Council to assist the Executive Office of the President
Ensure that Native Americans have opportunities to participate in federal economic development

topic.name: Abortion Legislation and Policies
topic.description: This cluster focuses on various legislative acts related to abortion, including restrictions, funding, and education regarding abortion and reproductive health.

Document 1: Communities of Color Teenage Pregnancy Prevention Act of 2011
Requires the Secretary to award grants for multimedia campaigns to provide public education regarding teenage pregnancy.
Document 2: Unborn Child Pain Awareness Act of 2010
Requires an abortion provider to inform the woman of the probable age of the child.
Document 3: No Taxpayer Funding for Abortion Act
Prohibits the expenditure of funds for any abortion or for health benefits coverage that includes coverage of abortion.
Document 4: Every Child is a Blessing Act of 2014
Prohibits recovery of damages in certain civil actions based on a claim that a child, once conceived, would not or should not have been born.
Document 6: Title X Abortion Provider Prohibition Act
Prohibits federal family planning assistance to an entity unless the entity certifies that it will not perform an abortion.
Document 7: Child Interstate Abortion Notification Act
Prohibits transporting a minor child across a state line to obtain an abortion.

topic.name: Postal Benefits for Armed Forces
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to providing postal benefits and services to members of the Armed Forces, particularly those serving overseas or hospitalized due to military service.

Document 1: Supply Our Soldiers Act of 2011 - Directs the Secretary of Defense (DOD) to provide for a program under which postal benefits are provided to a member of the Armed Forces who is on active duty.
Provides the postal benefits in the form of coupons or other evidence of credit (vouchers) to use for postal-free mailings.
Document 2: Home Front to Heroes Postal Benefits Act - Directs the Secretary of Defense to provide for a program under which postal benefit vouchers are provided during FY2010 for members of the Armed Forces who are serving in overseas operations.
Permits the vouchers to be transferred to another person to allow that person to mail correspondence and small parcels to the member.
Document 3: Supply Our Soldiers Act of 2015 - Directs the Department of Defense to provide for a program under which postal benefits are provided to a member of the Armed Forces who is on active duty.
Requires the postal benefits to be in the form of coupons, vouchers, or other evidence of credit to use for postal-free mailings.

topic.name: Water and Land Management Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on the management, reclamation, and environmental impacts of water resources and land use in the context of mining and public health.

Document 1: Amends the Reclamation Wastewater and Groundwater Study and Facilities Act to authorize the Secretary of the Interior to participate in the design, planning, and construction of recycled water distribution and wastewater treatment and reclamation facilities.
used to provide recycled water in that District.
Document 4: amends the USEC Privatization Act governing uranium transfers and sales to require the Department of Energy to issue a long-term excess uranium inventory management plan.
ensure that the federal government maximizes for itself the potential value of uranium.
Document 5: extends a state's shield from federal liability for costs or damages incurred as a result of actions taken or omitted while implementing a state abandoned mine reclamation plan.
include a state's own reclamation activities conducted pursuant to an approved plan.
Document 6: Requires the Director of the National Institute of Environmental Health Sciences to conduct or support comprehensive studies on the health impacts of mountaintop removal coal mining.
Prohibits issuance of an authorization for any mountaintop removal coal mining project until the Secretary publishes a determination that such mining does not present any health risk.
Document 7: Applies this Act to any mining claim, millsite claim, or tunnel site claim located under the general mining laws.
Prescribes requirements for the exploration permit process and for operations permits, including financial assurances.

topic.name: Commemoration and Recognition Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on honoring and recognizing individuals and events related to military service, public service, and national values.

Document 1: deceased fire fighter, law enforcement officer, emergency technician, or other rescue worker who died in the line of duty
Capitol-flown flag
Document 2: commemorate and honor those who served on active duty in support of Operation Desert Storm or Operation Desert Shield
establish a commemorative work on federal land
Document 4: Pledge of Allegiance to the Flag and the national motto 'In God We Trust'
ensuring religious references are included in exhibits
Document 5: a memorial honoring helicopter pilots and crew members who served on active duty during the Vietnam era
placed in Arlington National Cemetery
Document 6: Welcome Home Vietnam Veterans Day
honoring and recognizing the contributions of veterans who served in Vietnam
Document 7: establishes the Ronald Reagan Centennial Commission
plan and carry out activities to honor Ronald Reagan

topic.name: Workplace Safety Regulations
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at enhancing safety and health standards in various workplaces, particularly in mining and agricultural sectors.

Document 1: Amends the Federal Mine Safety and Health Act of 1977
Requires the Secretary to establish a publicly available electronic database containing the safety records of each mine.
Document 2: Amends the Consolidated Farm and Rural Development Act to expand eligibility for loans
Deems specified embedded entities eligible for farm ownership and operating loans.
Document 3: Requires the Secretary of Labor to promulgate an interim final standard regulating occupational exposure to combustible dust hazards
Requires such standard to provide requirements for hazard assessment to identify, evaluate, and control combustible dust hazards.
Document 4: Amends the Federal Mine Safety and Health Act of 1977
Requires an independent accident investigation by an independent panel appointed by the Secretary of Health and Human Services.
Document 5: Amends the Mineral Leasing Act to require a person with the legal right to conduct oil and gas operations to compensate the surface owner for damages
Requires the operator to reclaim the affected surface.
Document 6: Directs the Department of Transportation to require the person purchasing or operating a gas or hazardous liquid pipeline facility to inspect it
The inspection requirement shall apply with respect to newly listed abandoned pipelines.

topic.name: Economic Assistance and Compensation Regulation
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at regulating economic assistance programs, compensation disclosure, and tax credits to support various groups, including military families, the unemployed, and vulnerable youth.

Document 1: establish an Internet website containing a searchable database of the executive compensation
Requires any institution receiving any TARP assistance to disclose annually to the Secretary the total compensation
Document 2: Subjects an individual's emergency unemployment compensation (EUC) benefit payment to a gradual reduction
the amount of EUC payable to any individual reduced by 5%
Document 3: Prioritizes... the Secretary of Defense... to pay members of the Armed Forces the earned pay
Appropriates to the Secretary of Defense and the Secretary of Homeland Security, for any period of lapsed appropriations, such amounts as may be necessary
Document 4: amends the Higher Education Act of 1965 to revise provisions related to financial aid and educational programs
expands the duties of the Student Loan Ombudsman to include the review and resolution of complaints
Document 5: allows employers a work opportunity tax credit for hiring long-term unemployed individuals
Defines 'long-term unemployed individual' as an individual certified as receiving federal or state unemployment compensation
Document 6: deny employers a tax deduction for payments of excessive compensation
require such employers to file a report with the Secretary of the Treasury on excessive compensation
Document 7: Makes any person or entity that received compensation for campaign-related work... ineligible to receive funds from the American Recovery and Reinvestment Act
requires such person or entity to return the ARRA funds, with interest, to the Secretary of the Treasury

topic.name: Legal Reforms and Regulations
topic.description: This cluster discusses various legal reforms and regulations affecting law enforcement, education, environmental protection, and judicial systems in the United States.

Document 1: Amends the Indian Law Enforcement Reform Act to direct the Secretary of the Interior to submit to Congress a long-term plan to address incarceration in Indian country.
Establishes the Indian Law and Order Commission to conduct a comprehensive study of law enforcement and criminal justice in tribal communities.
Document 2: Amends the federal judicial code to declare that, with respect to diversity of citizenship, the U.S. district courts shall not have original jurisdiction of any civil action between citizens of a state and citizens or subjects of a foreign state.
Separates the removal requirements governing civil cases and those governing criminal cases into two separate categories.
Document 3: Directs the Secretary of Education to make annual determinations as to whether each state's public school system provides all its students with educational resources to succeed academically and in life.
Allows students or parents aggrieved by violations of this Act to bring civil actions for enforcement in federal district courts.
Document 4: Amends the Safe Drinking Water Act to authorize increased appropriations to the Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) for FY2009-FY2013 for technical assistance.
Requires that preference be given to nonprofit organizations determined to be qualified, effective, and to have the most support from small water systems in the states.
Document 5: Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to direct the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) to identify and establish standards for contaminants in bottled water.
Prohibits a manufacturer of bottled water from failing to submit an annual report or to display the required information on bottle labels in accordance with this Act.
Document 6: Withdraws approximately 512 acres of covered land in Arizona from all forms of entry, appropriation, or disposal under the public land laws.
Grants the Secretary of the Interior exclusive jurisdiction to manage the Craigin Project in accordance with the Arizona Water Settlements Act.
Document 7: Authorizes a six-month open enrollment period starting 30 days after enactment of this Act for a federal judicial official to opt into the Judicial Survivors' Annuities System.

Allows judicial officials enrolled in the System before or after enactment of this Act to purchase, in three-month increments, up to an additional year of service credit for each year of federal judicial service completed.

topic.name: Social Welfare Legislation
topic.description: This cluster discusses various legislative acts aimed at improving social welfare, including support for vulnerable populations such as veterans, children, and immigrants.

1: Establishes a congressional Commission on the Abolition of Modern-day Slavery.
3: Increases pension rates for veterans of a period of war.
Supports surviving spouses and children of veterans.
5: Directs the Secretary of Health and Human Services to make grants to improve quality of care for infants and toddlers.
Authorizes funds to support initiatives for high-quality infant and toddler care.
6: Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to exempt sons and daughters of Filipino World War II veterans from visa limitations.

topic.name: Child Safety Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on various legislative acts aimed at enhancing child safety in different contexts, including education, consumer products, and health.

Document 1: training of school personnel to recognize child sexual abuse
grant program under part A
Document 2: study drywall that was imported from China
consumer product safety standard regulating drywall materials
Document 3: liquid nicotine containers designed with special packaging
harmful contents from being accessed by children
Document 4: competitive grants for solar equipment
deployment of solar energy systems
Document 5: bans children's product containing hazardous substances
CPSC to revise lead standards
Document 6: evaluate existing surveillance for traumatic brain injury
National Traumatic Brain Injury Surveillance System
Document 7: portable gasoline container child-resistance standard
CPSC to report on compliance and incidents

topic.name: Natural Disaster Management
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation and programs aimed at reducing risks and providing assistance related to natural disasters such as earthquakes, hurricanes, and agricultural impacts from extreme weather.

Document 1: Amends the Earthquake Hazards Reduction Act of 1977 to revise and reauthorize the National Earthquake Hazards Reduction Program
NIST responsible for research and development to improve building standards and practices.
Document 2: Directs the Secretary of Agriculture to provide financial assistance for crop disaster assistance for qualifying quantity or quality losses due to weather or related conditions
Includes livestock assistance for disaster-caused losses, including wildfires and floods.
Document 3: Authorizes the Administrator of NASA to procure and launch the next-generation Extended Ocean Vector Winds Mission
Requires development of a plan and schedule for the procurement and launch of the XOVWM constellation of satellites.
Document 4: Requires the National Science Foundation to establish a Global Warming Education Program to broaden the understanding of human induced global warming
Emphasizes actionable information to help people understand and promote implementation of new technologies.
Document 6: Requires the Administrator of NOAA to conduct a coordinated national ocean exploration program
Authorizes appropriations for undersea research, exploration, education, and technology development programs.

topic.name: Environmental Regulation and Safety
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at regulating environmental impacts and ensuring safety in various sectors, particularly in oil and gas operations and public safety measures.

Document 1: Amends the Outer Continental Shelf Lands Act
Requires an exploration plan to include a plan for drilling at least one emergency relief well
Document 2: Amends the Federal Water Pollution Control Act
Requires the Secretary of the Interior to study and report on stormwater impacts
Document 4: Directs the Administrator of the National Highway Traffic Safety Administration to carry out a collaborative research effort
Explore feasibility of in-vehicle technology to prevent alcohol-impaired driving
Document 5: Requires the Secretary of Agriculture to ensure that all pack and saddle stock uses are recognized
Prohibits actions limiting pack and saddle stock without environmental analysis
Document 6: Directs the Secretary of Defense to conduct a study on establishing a deep water sea port in the Arctic
Protect and advance U.S. strategic interests within the Arctic region
Document 7: Authorizes the Secretary of the Interior to provide financial assistance for a pilot program
Develop methods to eradicate or control feral swine and restore damaged wetlands

topic.name: Counterterrorism Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at combating terrorism and enhancing national security through various measures including the use of military force, restrictions on firearms, and drone assessments.

Document 1: Authorizes the President to use all necessary force against countries or persons associated with terrorist groups
intended to constitute specific statutory authorization pursuant to section 5(b) of the War Powers Resolution.
Document 2: allows the Department of Justice to establish a list of persons that have engaged in or supported terrorism
prohibit a person on the list from participating in the transfer of a firearm.
Document 3: research how commercially available small and medium sized unmanned aircraft could be used to perpetuate an attack
develop policies, guidance, and protocols for DHS to prevent and mitigate the risks of such an attack.
Document 5: grants the Attorney General the authority to deny the transfer of firearms to individuals engaged in terrorism
permits the Attorney General to withhold information in firearms and explosives license denial revocation suits if it compromises national security.

topic.name: Legislative Acts on Welfare and Health
topic.description: This cluster encompasses legislative acts focused on animal welfare, public health, and humanitarian relief efforts.

Document 1: Amends the Animal Welfare Act to list permissible sources of dogs and cats used by research facilities
Sets forth additional monetary penalties for related violations.
Document 2: Amends the Public Health Service Act to direct the Secretary of Health and Human Services
develop and implement an ongoing and sustainable national strategy for identifying and evaluating the health status and health care needs of NHOPI populations.
Document 3: Amends the Animal Welfare Act to list permissible sources of dogs and cats used by research facilities
Prohibits federal facilities from purchasing or otherwise acquiring dogs or cats for exhibition purposes.
Document 4: Amends the Tariff Act of 1930 and the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States
grant duty-free treatment of donated fire-fighting and rescue and relief equipment.
Document 5: Directs the President to transmit to Congress a report on the status of the implementation of the National HIV/AIDS Strategy
report on the status of international progress towards achieving universal access to HIV/AIDS treatment.
Document 6: This bill amends the Public Health Service Act to prohibit the transfer of fetal tissue in exchange for valuable consideration
including payments associated with the transportation, implantation, processing, preservation, quality control, or storage of human fetal tissue.
Document 7: Amends the Legislative Branch Appropriations Act, 1987 to allow the Clerk of the House of Representatives to donate computers
without regard to whether the donation is a cost to the government.

topic.name: Consumer Protection Legislation
topic.description: A collection of legislative acts aimed at protecting consumers from unfair practices and ensuring transparency in various markets.

Document 1: Preserving American Homeownership Act of 2013 requires pilot programs to encourage shared appreciation mortgage modifications.
Result in positive net present value for the investor.
Document 2: Pink Tax Repeal Act prohibits pricing differences based on gender.
Violations treated as unfair or deceptive acts.
Document 3: Cyber Privacy Act requires websites to provide means for individuals to request removal of personal information.
Treats violations as unfair and deceptive acts.
Document 4: Incorporation Transparency and Law Enforcement Assistance Act mandates disclosure of beneficial ownership information.
Prescribes penalties for false or fraudulent information.
Document 5: Video Game Decency Act makes it unlawful to distribute video games with misleading content ratings.
Treats violations as unfair or deceptive acts.
Document 6: Consumer Financial Protection Agency Act establishes an agency to regulate consumer financial products.
Promotes transparency, simplicity, fairness, accountability, and access.
Document 7: Health Care Choice Act of 2015 amends health insurance laws to restore previous provisions.
Exempts issuers from certain state laws.

topic.name: Federal Legislation and Policy Changes
topic.description: This cluster encompasses various federal legislative acts that address issues related to national security, veterans' benefits, immigration policy, environmental regulations, and energy revenue sharing.

Document 1: Prohibits construing either the national monument designation or any management plan in furtherance of the Organ Mountains-Desert Peaks National Monument... as preventing the Department of Homeland Security... from undertaking law enforcement and border security activities.
preventing DHS from conducting low-level overflights over the Monument for law enforcement and border security purposes.
Document 2: Directs the Secretary of Defense (DOD) to permit any former member of the armed forces who is entitled to compensation for a service-connected disability rated total... to travel on military aircraft.
Document 3: Prohibits a federal agency or instrumentality from using federal funding or resources after July 30, 2014, to... consider or adjudicate any new or previously denied application of any alien requesting consideration of deferred action for childhood arrivals...
authorize any alien to work in the United States who was not lawfully admitted into the United States.
Document 4: Requires the Administrator of the Federal Emergency Management Agency (FEMA) to reimburse an eligible entity... that incurs expenses for the services of a surveyor, engineer, or similar service provider...
in obtaining a letter of map amendment that is approved in whole or in part by the Administrator.
Document 5: This bill amends the National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2013 to permit an affected eligible beneficiary who was enrolled in TRICARE Prime... to make the one-time election to continue TRICARE Prime coverage.
Makes this Act effective as if included in the enactment of the National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2014.
Document 6: Provides that: (1) the final rule published by the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA)... shall have no force or effect with respect to existing stationary compression ignition reciprocating internal combustion engines...
and any subsequent regulation that is substantially the same shall have no force or effect...
Document 7: Amends the Outer Continental Shelf Lands Act to require the Secretary of the Treasury to deposit in the Treasury 37.5% of all revenues derived from all rentals, royalties, bonus bids...
Requires the Secretary of the Interior to disperse: (1) 27.5% of these revenues to coastal states and their political subdivisions.

topic.name: Human Rights and Military Oversight
topic.description: This cluster addresses the intersection of human rights, military engagement, and legislative oversight concerning various nations.

Document 1: Prohibits FY2014 Department of Defense (DOD) funds from being made available to the government of Burma unless the Secretary of State certifies to Congress that Burma has taken steps toward establishing civilian oversight of the armed forces.
Burma has taken steps to establish a fair and inclusive process to amend the Constitution of Burma, including the full participation of the political opposition and ethnic minority groups.
Document 5: Establishes a Congressional-Executive Commission on the Socialist Republic of Vietnam, which shall monitor the Socialist Republic of Vietnam's human rights compliance.

Maintain lists of persons believed to be imprisoned, detained, tortured, or otherwise persecuted by the government of the Socialist Republic of Vietnam due to their pursuit of the rights described in this Act.
Document 6: Declares that the right to life guaranteed by the Constitution is vested in each human and is the person's paramount and most fundamental right.
Each human life begins with fertilization, cloning, or its functional equivalent, at which time every human has all legal and constitutional attributes and privileges of personhood.

topic.name: Government Accountability and Oversight
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at enhancing government accountability, oversight, and regulatory processes across various sectors.

Document 1: Amends False Claims Act liability provisions
Allows the government to move to dismiss a qui tam relator
Document 2: Amends the Federal Financial Institutions Examination Council Act
Grants a financial institution the right to appeal a material supervisory determination
Document 3: Requires the National Archives and Records Administration to establish the Missing Armed Forces Personnel Records Collection
Establishes the Missing Armed Forces Personnel Records Review Board
Document 4: Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to require an application for approval for a new drug
Establishes a Drug Safety Oversight Board
Document 5: Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to eliminate the time limit on the filing of a motion to reopen a removal proceeding
Document 6: Amends the Consumer Financial Protection Act of 2010 to authorize any person to require the CFPB to terminate a proceeding
Provides a framework for civil actions against individuals
Document 7: Makes it unlawful for any state or political subdivision to restrict information regarding an individual's citizenship
Violations lead to ineligibility for federal assistance programs

topic.name: Federal Legislative Acts
topic.description: A collection of legislative acts focused on various federal programs and regulations across education, wildlife conservation, homeland security, food safety, environmental protection, and procurement.

Document 1: Amends the Impact Aid program to authorize grants to LEAs for federally-connected children due to military activities.
Document 2: Amends the Pittman-Robertson Wildlife Restoration Act to require funds for wildlife conservation.
Document 3: Requires DHS to report on international vetting and screening strategies for persons entering the U.S.
Document 4: Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to expand food safety activities.
Document 5: Amends the Clean Water Act to regulate silvicultural activities without requiring certain permits.
Document 6: Amends the Buy American Act to require compliance evaluations for federal contracts.
Document 7: Prohibits construction of the Keystone XL Pipeline without compliance with specified permits.

topic.name: Healthcare and Education Policy
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative measures aimed at improving access to healthcare and education, including loan repayment programs and funding for primary care training.

Document 1: authorize the Secretary of the Interior to create and operate within the available capacity of Navajo Reservoir a top water bank
construct, operate, and maintain the Navajo Indian Irrigation Project
Document 2: test models for providing payment for direct graduate medical education and indirect medical education
collaborating with two or more hospitals to operate one or more primary care graduate medical residency training programs
Document 3: establish a 10/10 Loan Repayment Plan
includes primary care physicians in medically underserved areas in the public service employee loan forgiveness program
Document 4: include the District of Columbia as an eligible jurisdiction under a grant program for states to provide educational loan repayment to health professionals
provision of primary health services in health professional shortage areas
Document 5: establishes the Consumer Credit Safety Commission to promulgate consumer credit safety rules
ban abusive, fraudulent, unfair, deceptive, predatory practices
Document 6: extend the eligibility period for a 'Welcome to Medicare' initial preventive physical examination
eliminate the 20% coinsurance for screening mammography and colorectal cancer screening tests
Document 7: require a private educational lender to include in a private education loan for which the cosigner is jointly liable a process for releasing the cosigner from obligations on the loan

prohibit consumer reporting agencies from containing in any consumer report default on a private education loan that results from accelerated repayment terms of the loan after the death or bankruptcy of a jointly liable cosigner

topic.name: U.S. Legislative Acts on Land and Security
topic.description: This cluster encompasses various U.S. legislative acts related to land management, national security, and resource allocation.

Document 1: Authorizes the Secretary of Agriculture to sell certain lands in Superior National Forest in Minnesota.
Requires consideration for a sale of lands to be not less than the appraised market value of those lands.
Document 2: Sets the tonnage requirement for consideration of dredging of small ports by the Corps of Engineers at a minimum of 500,000 tons.
Document 3: Grants the consent and approval of Congress to the interstate forest fire protection compact for the Great Plains region.
Document 4: Amends the Controlled Substances Act to provide penalties for drug trafficking conspiracies outside the U.S.
Excludes simple possession of a controlled substance from the application of this Act.
Document 5: Adjusts the boundary of the Apostle Islands National Lakeshore in Wisconsin to include the Ashland Harbor Breakwater Light Addition.
Authorizes the Secretary to operate, maintain, and inspect any federal aid to navigation located at the Ashland Harbor Breakwater Light.
Document 6: Directs the Secretary of Homeland Security to submit a strategy for gaining operational control of the international borders.
Requires analysis of staffing requirements, infrastructure needs, and technology for border security.
Document 7: Prohibits the Director of the Federal Housing Finance Agency from disposing of real estate under bulk sale initiatives.
Specifically targets real estate owned by Fannie Mae and Freddie Mac located in California.

topic.name: Legislative Acts and Reforms
topic.description: This cluster encompasses various legislative acts aimed at reforming government operations, enhancing public safety, and improving healthcare and economic stability.

Document 1: Removing Limitations on Insurance Effectiveness and Flexibility Act of 2015 or the ReLIEF Act
deems catastrophic health plans described in the Patient Protection and Affordable Care Act to be qualified health plans
Document 2: United States Secret Service Uniformed Division Modernization Act of 2010
States as the purpose of this Act to transfer statutory entitlements to pay and hours of work for members of the U.S. Secret Service Uniformed Division
Document 3: Help Find the Missing Act or Billy's Law
Authorizes the Attorney General to maintain public databases, known as the National Missing and Unidentified Persons System or NamUs
Document 4: Special Inspector General for the Troubled Asset Relief Program Act of 2009
grants the Special Inspector General (SIG) authority to conduct, supervise, and coordinate an audit or investigation
Document 5: Keep Out the IRS Act of 2011
Prohibits the Internal Revenue Service (IRS) from filling any position
Document 6: Federal Executive Board Authorization Act of 2009
Requires the Director of the Office of Personnel Management (OPM) to establish Federal Executive Boards
Document 7: Removes the requirement that the person detailed as Chief of the Navy Dental Corps be of a rank not less than rear admiral
Requires an appointee to such position holding a lower grade to be appointed in the regular grade of rear admiral

topic.name: Renewable Energy and Economic Development
topic.description: The documents collectively address legislation related to the promotion of renewable energy technologies and regional economic development initiatives.

Document 1: Marine and hydrokinetic renewable energy technology research, development, demonstration, and commercial application
Requires the Department of Energy (DOE) to award grants to support modifying or constructing marine and hydrokinetic renewable energy technology research facilities.
Document 2: Establishes the Northern Border Economic Development Commission to develop plans and programs for economic development.
Requires each state to submit a development plan for the area of the region represented by such state.
